name: Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop
name_original: Nuévalos - Milmarcos - Calmarza - Jaraba — circular desde Ibdes
description: Cycle 37.4 miles (60.2 km) on this moderate circular route from Ibdes, exploring Nuévalos, Milmarcos, Calmarza, and Jaraba.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ibdes, located in the Comunidad de Calatayud in Zaragoza, Aragon, Spain, offer diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is shaped by the Mesa River, which has created undulating roads, ascents, and descents, providing engaging challenges. This riverine influence also contributes to scenic routes with natural geological formations and fertile lands. The area features varied elevations suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Ibdes The most popular road cycling route is Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features moderate climbs and views of the Embalse de la Tranquera. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Nuévalos - Monterde -, a moderate 39.7 miles (63.9 km) path. This route connects towns through varied terrain, including significant elevation gains. Local road cyclists also love the Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and around the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Ibdes is defined by river valleys, undulating roads, and significant ascents, with routes that cater to both moderate and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Ibdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ibdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ibdes?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Ibdes, offering diverse options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 5 reviews.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Ibdes?
A: The terrain around Ibdes is shaped by the Mesa River, resulting in undulating roads, ascents, and descents. You can expect varied elevations, from moderate climbs to more challenging sections, providing engaging rides through natural geological formations and fertile lands.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Ibdes?
A: Road cycling routes in Ibdes cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 12 routes classified as moderate and 8 routes considered difficult, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Ibdes?
A: Yes, many routes around Ibdes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic circular ride.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes near Ibdes?
A: The region offers several natural attractions. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de la Tranquera&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that lead near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreaderos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Mesa River itself creates picturesque valleys and ravines to enjoy.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike?
A: While cycling, you can pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Monastery Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, which offer interesting stops. The region's towns and villages also provide glimpses into local culture.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ibdes?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, Spain's Aragon region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners around Ibdes?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate or difficult, the region's diverse terrain means there are sections that are less demanding. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31469944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuévalos loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 48 km with moderate elevation gains.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Ibdes area?
A: For a more challenging ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; features significant elevation changes over its 60 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989969946/nuevalos-monterde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuévalos - Monterde -&lt;/a&gt; route, which includes substantial climbs over its 63.9 km.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ibdes?
A: The komoot community rates road cycling in Ibdes highly, with an average of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which offers both engaging challenges and scenic views, particularly along the river valleys and around the reservoirs.

Q: Are there routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Embalse de la Tranquera, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you directly around the reservoir.

Q: What is the average duration for road cycling routes in Ibdes?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993007242/camino-del-cid-and-embalse-de-la-tranquera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid and Embalse de la Tranquera Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours 10 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991320802/tranquera-reservoir-and-mesa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranquera Reservoir and Mesa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in around 3 hours 4 minutes.
tips: Nice views of Nuévalos and the Tranquera reservoir in the background, from the road reaching the town. If we are lucky enough to see the reservoir with its well-charged water reserves, we will see its spectacular emerald blue color. We will find fishermen, people bathing and a lot of fauna.
We are next to the castle of Calmarza, where we can see a stately tower whose origin is unknown. It is 12 meters tall.
An essential corner for nature lovers. The "Pozo Redondo" Waterfall of Calmarza is a charming place that is hidden in the lower part of the town where the Mesa River passes

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for traditional road bikes?
faqs.answer: While Komoot classifies this as a 'racebike' route, our research indicates that it is unlikely to be fully paved. You should expect mixed terrain, including unpaved segments, gravel, and dirt tracks, especially between towns like Jaraba and Calmarza. These sections can be challenging or unsuitable for narrow road bike tires. A gravel bike or mountain bike would be more appropriate for this adventure.

faqs.question: How challenging is this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the strong likelihood of unpaved sections and the region's mountainous terrain, this route would be classified as hard for road cyclists. It features significant elevation changes and potentially rough surfaces, demanding good physical fitness and bike handling skills. Be prepared that some parts might require dismounting and walking if you are on a traditional road bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied terrain. While some segments may be paved, many parts are likely to be unpaved, featuring gravel, dirt tracks, and rough surfaces. The area is known for its diverse landscapes, including rugged mountains and river valleys, so be ready for a mix of surfaces and gradients.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll get a fantastic View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir. Further along, you can see the historic Calmarza Tower, and the beautiful Pozo Redondo Waterfall. The towns themselves, like Jaraba with its thermal spas, also offer charming rural Spanish landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in this region of Aragon is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Ibdes?
faqs.answer: As the route is a circular one starting from Ibdes, you should be able to find parking within the town center or its vicinity. Look for designated public parking areas in Ibdes to safely leave your vehicle before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas and villages, its suitability for dogs depends on your cycling setup and your dog's endurance. Given the mixed terrain and potential for unpaved sections, it might be challenging for dogs to run alongside for the entire 60km. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are comfortable with long distances and varied surfaces, or consider using a bike trailer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths connecting the towns of Nuévalos, Milmarcos, Calmarza, Jaraba, and Ibdes.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns including Nuévalos, Milmarcos, Calmarza, Jaraba, and Ibdes. You should find cafes, restaurants, and small shops in these villages where you can stop for refreshments, a meal, or to refill your water bottles.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will cycle along parts of the Jaraba-Calmarza trail, the Ibdes-Jaraba trail, and the Caminos de Labros y Santa Catalina. Other smaller segments include Llumes - Calmarza and Senda a la Cueva y Fuente del Moro, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery characteristic of Aragon, Spain. This includes rugged mountains, river valleys, and potentially dramatic gorges, especially around Jaraba and Calmarza. The route offers a blend of natural beauty and views of charming rural Spanish villages.

waypoints.name: View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir
